description: Page navigation landmark. The root is a <nav role="navigation" aria-label="pagination"> containing an <ul> of PaginationItem elements. PaginationLink is an <a> styled as a button; the current page is marked with aria-current="page". PaginationPrevious and PaginationNext add descriptive aria-labels ("Go to previous page" / "Go to next page").

keyboard.action: Moves focus to the next pagination link.
Moves focus to the previous pagination link.
Activates the focused link (navigates to that page).

focus.notes: Uses the Button component's focus-visible ring (since PaginationLink applies buttonVariants). Ellipsis elements are aria-hidden and not focusable.

announcements: Each page link announces its number; the current page is marked aria-current="page".
Previous / Next links announce their aria-labels for context.
Ellipsis items render an sr-only "More pages" so screen readers don't jump from 5 to 42 without warning.
screenReader.notes: The component does not track page state; it only renders the UI. Wire the active page via the isActive prop on the relevant PaginationLink.

contrast.text: Link text uses the Button ghost / outline variants; both meet WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Active link's outline variant meets AA non-text contrast against bg-background

motion.reducedMotion: Only the standard color transition on hover; no motion-based animations

consumerResponsibilities: Set isActive on the current page's PaginationLink so aria-current is emitted.
Provide real href values so links work without JS; if you use a router, compose via asChild / the router's Link component.
For long ranges, use PaginationEllipsis between non-adjacent pages; don't render all 200 page numbers.
Consider a visible "Page X of Y" summary nearby; the component does not generate one.
